summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a GPG release signing feature, then add a user-facing verification script that fetches the maintainer's GPG key from https://pen.so/gpg.asc.
summary.outcome: All three review threads were resolved (SHA256 skip warning, clobber notice, fingerprint source docs), a new verify-release.sh script was created and committed, and the signing script and verification docs were updated to reference the new script and key URL.

repo: The moltis repo uses worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/ for PR branch isolation.
GPG signing scripts live in scripts/ and documentation in docs/src/; both are updated together when adding signing/verification features.
PR #525 is on the moltis-org/moltis GitHub repo and uses GraphQL for resolving review threads.

code.finding: Signing script was updated to mention verify-release.sh in its final output, coupling signing and verification workflows.
New script created to verify GPG signatures, fetching the maintainer's public key from https://pen.so/gpg.asc.
Docs reference both keys.openpgp.org and the maintainer's GitHub GPG keys page as key sources, plus the new verify script.

workflow: GitHub GraphQL API was used to check for unresolved review threads before pushing — a useful pattern to avoid redundant work.
Review threads were resolved via the GitHub API directly from the CLI after addressing each comment in code.

friction: A second pass to check for 'new comments' found no new threads — the user may have expected new comments that hadn't been posted yet, causing a redundant check cycle.
open_items: The verify-release.sh script fetches the key from pen.so/gpg.asc at runtime — no offline/cached key fallback is handled.
SHA256 integrity check in the signing script warns but skips when no checksum is present; a stricter enforcement mode could be considered later.
